# Principles of Fire Protection Engineering

## Description:

This three- day course is open to all individuals interested in gaining or refreshing their basic to intermediate knowledge of the principles of fire protection engineering. This course presents the application of science and engineering principles to protect people, property, and their environments from harmful and destructive effects of fire and smoke. It covers areas of fire detection, suppression, and mitigation as well as human behavior and recommendations on how to maintain a tenable environment for evacuation during a fire scenario. Fire suppression systems components will be examined to include fire science, fire safety design, fire detection and fire alarm systems, fire suppression systems, automatic sprinkler systems and smoke control principles. Industry standards and variations will be discussed using NFPA codes and building codes.

## Learning Objectives

Upon completion of this course participants will be able to:

## Prerequisite

None.

## Target Audience

Fire Protection Engineers (FPEs), Fire Alarm Designers, Architectural, Civil, Structural, Mechanical and Electrical Engineers.

## Course Assessment

Participants will be assessed via a written exam. A passing score of 70% will be required to obtain a Certificate of Completion.

--- SOURCE 3: ISO 23932:2009 - Fire safety engineering ---

SUMMARY:
ISO 23932:2009 provides general principles for a performance-based methodology for engineers to assess the level of fire safety for new or existing built

FULL CONTENT:
![ISO logo for print](/modules/isoorg-template/img/iso/iso-logo-print.gif)
![](/modules/iso-jahia-service-module/img/iso-logo.png)
![](/modules/iso-jahia-service-module/img/iso-standard-cover-thumbnail.png)

# ISO 23932:2009 Fire safety engineering — General principles

## Abstract

ISO 23932:2009 provides general principles for a performance-based methodology for engineers to assess the level of fire safety for new or existing built environments. Fire safety is evaluated through an engineered approach based on the quantification of the behaviour of fire and people and based on knowledge of the consequences of such behaviour on life safety, property and the environment.

ISO 23932:2009 is not intended as a detailed technical design guide, but does contain the key elements needed by practicing fire safety engineers and peer reviewers (those entities who can be required to review the work of fire-safety engineers) for addressing the different steps and their linkages in a design process. The information contained in ISO 23932:2009 is intended not only to be useful to engineers directly but also to serve as a template to guide the development of a consistent set of fire-safety engineering documents covering the role of engineering methods and test methods in performance-based design and assessment.

The basic principles of fire-safety design and related fire-safety objectives in ISO 23932:2009 can be applied in any other document addressing phenomena associated with fire (e.g. fire growth, hot gases and effluents movement, structural and compartmentalization behaviour). Related fire-safety objectives include, for example, safety of life; conservation of property; continuity of operations; protection of the environment; preservation of heritage.

Furthermore, these basic principles can be applied to all configurations of the built environment (e.g. buildings, transportation systems and industrial installations).

Because prescriptive regulations covering fire-safety design will co-exist for some time with performance-based design, ISO 23932:2009 takes into account that fire-safety designs conforming to prescriptive regulations can become the basis for comparison of engineered designs of new built environments.

--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.modernbuildingalliance.us/wp-content/uploads/common-principles-1024x858.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the key principles of fire safety engineering, including prevention, detection and communication, occupant protection, containment, and extinguishment, arranged in a circular flow to emphasize their interconnected roles.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/71syDs5BJDL._SL1400_.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A fire engine is spraying water on a burning building with thick smoke, emphasizing fire safety principles and fire protection measures.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/images/S/compressed.photo.goodreads.com/books/1434539648i/25743259.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The cover features a firefighter using a ladder to spray water on a burning building, along with images of fire detection and suppression equipment like a fire alarm, sprinkler system, and fire pump, emphasizing key principles of fire safety engineering.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/firesafetyprinciples-240116024337-61e689a9/75/Fire-safety-Principles-for-building-spaces-33-2048.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates key fire safety engineering principles, including site access for fire control with unobstructed routes, traffic control measures such as bollards and fences, fire hydrant placement and connection, use of T-turns and cul-de-sacs to prevent hazards, and the importance of unobstructed fire hoses and strategic placement of safety equipment and infrastructure.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/firesafetyprinciples-240116024337-61e689a9/75/Fire-safety-Principles-for-building-spaces-35-2048.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image outlines the basic principles of fire safety engineering, focusing on the fire fighting shaft's role in providing access, fire-rated doors, and being fully equipped with firefighting equipment, service elevator, stair, and lobby in high-rise buildings.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Fire safety engineering emphasizes prevention, detection, communication, occupant protection, containment, and extinguishment, ensuring a comprehensive approach to fire hazards.

enriched_points.point_title: Role of engineering in fire prevention
enriched_points.expanded_content: Engineering integrates scientific principles to design systems that minimize fire risks, enhance detection, and ensure effective communication during emergencies, safeguarding lives and property.
Ongoing research drives innovations in fire safety technologies and methodologies, improving design standards and enhancing safety protocols for diverse built environments.
